candidate_lesson.lesson_text: When the 30-indicator consensus shows high conviction but green_matches=0 and hist_hr=0%, the live signal has no historical analog and must be sized down or vetoed regardless of conviction.
candidate_lesson.mechanism: Conviction measures live indicator agreement, but green_matches counts how many times a similar indicator configuration existed in the historical memory bank and made money. When green_matches=0 the current setup is a novel configuration with no proven institutional follow-through — high conviction on an unprecedented pattern is overfitting to present noise, not detecting a repeatable flow regime. AUDNZD SHORT (conv=100%, green_matches=0, hist_hr=0%) and USDJPY LONG (conv=50%, green_matches=0, hist_hr=0%) are exactly these traps.
candidate_lesson.testable_rule: if consensus_conviction >= 0.90 and green_matches == 0: size *= 0.0  # veto; elif green_matches == 0 and hist_hr == 0: size *= 0.5
candidate_lesson.expected_impact: Removes zero-analog trades that dilute win rate; expected Sharpe +0.02 to +0.03 by cutting variance from unprecedented-config entries.
candidate_lesson.evidence_from_harvest: Live consensus shows AUDNZD conv=100% but green_matches=0/hist_hr=0%, and USDJPY conv=50% green_matches=0/hist_hr=0% — both high/moderate conviction with no historical winning analog, while GBPUSD (green_matches=2, hist_hr=66%) and AUDUSD (green_matches=3, hist_hr=68%) show conviction backed by proven analogs.

retirement_flag.reason: VIX is 16.5 and falling (contango, not rising) — L8's 'tighten gate when VIX rising' condition is currently inactive, so it is not gating anything in this regime; monitor whether it still fires when VIX crosses 13 to confirm it hasn't become dormant.

reflection.system_health_note: Zero trades in 7 days with regime=UNKNOWN and TradingView showing 0 signals means the system is in a low-conviction observation window — sizing at 75% is appropriate, but the empty decision log means all lessons remain harvest-only until execution resumes.
